concept: Taste / To Perceive with the Mouth
description: The act of gustatory perception, emphasizing a direct, embodied way of knowing and experiencing the world through the mouth.
rationale: The word 'mala' is sonically simple and evocative of the mouth. The initial 'ma-' uses the embodied nasal 'm' and open 'a' to suggest taking something in (Pre-industrial). The second syllable '-la' uses the liquid 'l' to represent the fluid sensation of taste. The repeating 'a' vowel gives the word a pure, harmonious feel, reflecting the directness of the experience (Art Nouveau).
